meta.description: Perpetual scalping knowledge: continuous, systematic scalping cycles with strict rules for entries, exits, and session management.

definition: Perpetual scalping = trading a continuous loop of small, high-probability setups on low timeframes (M1/M5), repeatedly harvesting small moves in the direction of a confirmed higher-timeframe bias, all session, every session, with fixed rules.

theLoop.description: The perpetual scalping cycle. Repeat continuously while session limits allow.
cycle: 1. BIAS: Re-check M15/H1 bias every 15 minutes. Only scalp in bias direction.
2. ZONE: Wait for price to reach a pre-marked M5 zone (S/R, order block, EMA21, VWAP).
3. TRIGGER: Wait for M1 confirmation pattern (engulfing, pin bar, break-retest).
4. EXECUTE: Enter with fixed risk (0.5-1%), SL beyond structure, TP at 1:1.5 to 1:2.
5. MANAGE: Move SL to breakeven at 1:1. No hoping, no widening stops.
6. LOG: Record the trade in the journal (self-learning.json schema).
7. RESET: Return to step 1. If 2 consecutive losses -> mandatory 30 min pause.
8. STOP: Hard stop at daily loss limit (-3%) or daily target (+5%). Session over.

entries: Only trade WITH the M15/H1 bias unless the counter-trend module explicitly validates a reversal (5+ confluences).
Minimum 3 confluences per entry — no exceptions for scalps.
Never chase: if the trigger candle already ran more than 50% to TP, skip.
Never enter during a 3xATR impulse candle; wait for the pullback.
One position per symbol at a time.
exits: TP is pre-defined; never remove it.
Time stop: if the scalp has not moved in your favour within 10 candles, exit.
Exit immediately if bias flips on M15 (CHoCH against you).
session: Max daily loss: 3% of account. Hit it -> stop, no re-entry.
Daily target: 5%. Hit it -> stop or reduce to quarter size.
After 2 consecutive losses: 30 minute mandatory break.

bestSetups.name: EMA Pullback Scalp
steps: M15 in clear trend (price above/below EMA50, ADX > 25).
M1 price pulls back to EMA21.
M1 prints rejection candle (pin/engulfing) at EMA21 in trend direction.
Enter on candle close. SL beyond pullback swing. TP 1:2.

bestSetups.name: Break-Retest Scalp
steps: M5 range or level breaks WITH a strong close and volume.
Wait for retest of the broken level.
M1 rejection at the retest -> enter in break direction.
SL beyond the level; TP = range height projected.

bestSetups.name: VWAP Bounce Scalp
steps: Trending market, price extended from VWAP.
Wait for return to VWAP with slowing momentum.
Rejection candle at VWAP in trend direction -> enter.
SL past VWAP band, TP at previous extreme.

bestSetups.name: Post-Spike Drift Scalp
steps: Boom/Crash only. Immediately after a spike prints.
Enter WITH the drift (sell Boom, buy Crash) on first M1 rejection.
Small TP (drift candles are small), exit before mid-cycle.

bestSetups.name: Range Edge Scalp
steps: Buy range low / sell range high only with rejection pattern + RSI extreme.
TP mid-range or opposite edge. Stop trading the range after a confirmed breakout.

psychology: Scalping is execution of a checklist, not prediction. Confidence comes from the process.
Every skipped bad trade is a win.
Never increase size after a loss (no martingale, ever).
The loop only compounds if losses stay small — protecting capital IS the strategy.

mathOfScalping.expectancy: E = (winRate x avgWin) - (lossRate x avgLoss). Must be positive after costs.
mathOfScalping.example: 50% win rate at 1:2 R:R = (0.5 x 2R) - (0.5 x 1R) = +0.5R per trade.
mathOfScalping.costs: Spread matters enormously on M1. Only scalp symbols where TP >= 10x spread.
